title: Artifacts remain usable when the configured baseline cannot be resolved
reasoning: Both freshness checks call resolveRef, but only reject a mismatched baselineSha when the configured baseline resolves successfully. If git rev-parse fails because the repo is not a git repository, the baseline ref is invalid, or git cannot verify it, baselineSha-bearing artifacts skip the stale checks and can still pass metadata validation. These functions are documented as fail-closed gates for stale or invalid artifacts, so inability to verify the trusted baseline should not make a recorded SHA trustworthy.
recommendation: Treat an unresolved configured baseline as unusable whenever the artifact relies on baselineSha freshness. Return a stale or invalid reason before accepting fence or calibration artifacts, and add the same fail-closed behavior anywhere baselineSha freshness is required.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing tests create a valid temporary git repository and always use baseline HEAD, so resolveRef succeeds in the covered paths. There is no test for an invalid baseline ref or non-git repo with a baselineSha-bearing artifact.
suggestedRegressionTest: Create a temp directory with a syntactically valid fence and calibration artifact containing baselineSha, configure baseline to a missing ref such as refs/heads/nope, and assert the statuses are unusable rather than accepted.
minimumFixScope: Update fenceArtifactStatus and calibrationArtifactStatus baseline resolution handling, plus focused tests for unresolved baseline refs.
